page: Amazon Web Services c8gb.2xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. Built on the arm64 architecture, this instance features 8 dedicated physical cores utilizing the ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 model running at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. The instance operates on the Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage options. Network performance is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 17 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong single-threaded performance. The instance achieves top-tier single-threaded bzip3 decompression speeds of 14.48 GB/sec and strong single-threaded gzip compression at 30.28 GB/sec. Geekbench testing shows a strong single-core score of 1890.0 and a strong text processing score of 2337.0. Multi-core performance is average, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 13645.12 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 10105.0. Memory bandwidth is measured at 924.95 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, dropping to 154.43 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads.

The hardware profile presents specific performance tradeoffs. While cryptographic operations are supported by hardware-accelerated AES, the Passmark CPU encryption test registers in the bottom 25% with a score of 1506.69. Additionally, large-model LLM inference is constrained; prompt processing for a medium 7B model is poor at 15.52 tokens/sec, though text generation for the same model remains average at 11.46 tokens/sec. The instance delivers cost efficiency for compute-heavy workloads that benefit from dedicated ARMv9 physical cores without hyperthreading, making it suitable for web serving, software compilation, and single-threaded data compression tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gb.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated physical cores on the arm64 ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 architecture running at 2.8 GHz. It is configured with 16.0 GB of memory, yielding 2.0 GB per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 17 Gbps. The instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware. Benchmarks reveal strong single-core capabilities, including top-tier single-threaded bzip3 decompression and strong single-threaded gzip compression. However, cryptographic encryption and medium-model LLM prompt processing show relative performance limitations. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading, making it highly suitable for software compilation, web serving, and text processing applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gb.2xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance with 8 dedicated Neoverse-V2 cores, 16 GB RAM, and 17 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong single-threaded performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8gb.2xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance with 8 dedicated physical cores, 16 GB RAM, and 17 Gbps baseline bandwidth.
tagline: An arm64 compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated physical cores and strong single-threaded performance for web serving and compilation workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 8 dedicated ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 physical cores at 2.8 GHz
Equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory and 17 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Delivers strong single-threaded compression and text processing performance
Lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on network storage
Suited for software compilation, web serving, and general compute workloads
